_captured_at_tag: @f.62 PR (taida-lang/taida#75) — refreshed from its first CI run
_captured_by: binary_size.yml run 27408804005 (GitHub ubuntu-latest); the previous @c.25.rc7 baseline (be235bb, 2026-04-23) absorbed seven generations of feature growth and the text section finally crossed the +10% budget at +10.88%

_host.note: CI-anchored baseline. `bytes` / `text` are reproducible at ±5%, `startup_ms` depends on runner I/O contention and can drift up to 10x run-to-run; the +10% budget absorbs that noise. Refresh by running scripts/measure_binary_size.sh --refresh-baseline on a GitHub runner (or re-extracting numbers from a .github/workflows/binary_size.yml run) and committing the result.

_policy: see docs/STABILITY.md §5.5 (performance is non-contractual). A +10% regression in any of bytes / text / startup_ms fails .github/workflows/binary_size.yml (continue-on-error: true). Hard-fail promotion is a post-stable follow-up. Refresh only with explicit maintainer approval.
